question:
Statements: 1. All cats are dogs. 2. All dogs are birds.
Conclusions: I. All cats are birds. II. Some birds are cats.

options.A: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II follows
options.C: Only Conclusion II follows
options.D: Both Conclusions I and II follow

explanation: If all A are B and all B are C, then all A are C (I follows). Also, if all A are C, then some C are A (II follows).

question:
Statements: 1. All pens are pencils. 2. Some pencils are erasers.
Conclusions: I. Some pens are erasers. II. No pens are erasers.

options.A: Only Conclusion II follows
options.C: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II follows
options.D: Either Conclusion I or Conclusion II follows

explanation: This is a complementary pair (Some A are B / No A are B). Since no direct relation is given between pens and erasers, either one or the other must be true.

question:
Statements: 1. No tree is a flower. 2. All flowers are fruits.
Conclusions: I. Some fruits are not trees. II. No fruit is a tree.

options.A: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II follows
options.B: Both Conclusions I and II follow
options.C: Only Conclusion II follows

explanation: Since all flowers are fruits and no tree is a flower, the fruits that are flowers can never be trees. Hence, some fruits are not trees. Conclusion II is not definite.
